summary: Cursor ReadStream applies map function twice
Document options supported by the unified topology
description: The unified topology is still hidden behind an opt-in feature flag, however we don't currently document new options it supports in the {{MongoClient}} documentation. We should document these options, and add them to the list of supported options so no warnings are printed when they are used.

summary: Fix qs dependency for older node
description: https://evergreen.mongodb.com/task/mongo_node_driver_next_rhel70_argon_test_3.2_sharded_cluster_ce9b08e9cb0b49ead39530af67b2a410da992d35_20_04_14_11_05_17

summary: Reduce race conditions in SDAM error handling
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    This work has been spun off from DRIVERS-640.

Reduce race conditions in SDAM error handling. In summary, drivers will update their SDAM and error handling logic to:
- Add topologyVersion field to ServerDescription.
- Ignore stale errors based on generation number and topologyVersion.
- Add SDAM spec tests for topologyVersion comparison.

Spec PR: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/781

Spec commit:
Author:{'name': 'Shane Harvey', 'email': 'shane.harvey@mongodb.com', 'username': 'ShaneHarvey'}
Message: SPEC-1663 Reduce race conditions in SDAM error handling (#781)

Add topologyVersion field to ServerDescription.
Add SDAM spec tests for topologyVersion comparison.
Clients ignore stale errors based on generation number and topologyVersion.
Branch: master
https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/7dd9c008be6edaf65368787d5e91c2ac3aeb95b8
    See DRIVERS-1187 for updated details.

summary: Unknown topology should return empty array for server selection with read preference
description: The {{readPreferenceServerSelector}} fails to consider if the topology it is selecting from is {{Unknown}}. The selection should short circuit and return an empty set in this case.

summary: Confusing documentation for collection.aggregate collation option
Server session creation grows unbounded with every operation call
description: Currently a {{ServerSession}} is acquired for each {{ClientSession}} in the constructor, which means that every operation creates a session as soon as its called, potentially much earlier than the operation is actually run. DRIVERS-1030 proposes that server session acquisition be deferred until a connection has been checked out for operation execution. While I anticipate a more comprehensive solution for the Node.js driver when we tackle that ticket, today we can make server session acquisition "lazy" and gain the same benefit.

summary: promiseLibrary option is not honoured
description:
I've got a feeling that [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/commit/f6a8ceb0aa84a50a7aed14cd4d4d847a1b8e3f72#diff-af7f90364fd34f588f43f92c13a92ab6R217] has broken the promiseLibrary option.

 

For example, if I call the static MongoClient.connect with options including promiseLibrary set to bluebird, the promise returned is not a bluebird promise. I looks like maybePromise ([https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/utils.js#L699]) does not honour the promiseLibrary option.

summary: "language_override" option support for text index is broken
description:
Hi Mongo team,

on 3.6.0 driver in node.js, the language override option when creating text search index doesnt work anymore, instead of setting the specified value, it always set the default value, which is "language"

If this is the case, the last version of the node.js driver introduces a major issue.

[https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/tutorial/specify-language-for-text-index/#use-any-field-to-specify-the-language-for-a-document]

summary: Server_Description update with lastUpdateTime / lastWriteDate fields  is ignored in topology
description:
We found and issue with 3.5.3 driver and unifiedTopology = true:

Server description with lastUpdateTime / lastWriteDate fields updated is ignored in topology.

 

By [Spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-discovery-and-monitoring/server-discovery-and-monitoring.rst#parsing-an-ismaster-response] Server_DEscription must be update with new one on every isMaster response.

But [Topology|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/v3.5.3/lib/core/sdam/topology.js#L524] ignores Server_Description if previous Server_Description considered equal to new one.

[Server_Description::equals()|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/v3.5.3/lib/core/sdam/server_description.js#L122]does not account for lastWriteDate lastUpdateTime and some other fields.

 

This can cause bugs when [selecting server by staleness|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/v3.5.3/lib/core/sdam/server_selection.js#L52]

 

We found issue by attaching to working server and adding breakpoints to Topology::serverUpdateHandler() and Topology_Description::update().

Topology_Description::update() was not called for 30 minuted. and Topology::serverUpdateHandler() was constantly called with new Server_Description but it never updated description cause previous one was always considered equal to new one.

 

This behavior was introduced in 3.3.4 driver

summary: createCollection helper should not run listIndexes outside of strict mode
description: Our {{createCollections}} helper has a concept of a "strict" mode, where the collection name is checked before attempting the create in order to prevent duplicate creation. The current implementation of the helper _always_ runs a {{listCollections}} and returns a cached version of the collection if it already exists, even if the options for the operation are different.

summary: Memory Leak with ReplSet and NodeJS v12
description:
Env:
 * node js 12.16.0
 * mongodb version (3.5.3, 3.2.7, 3.1.10)
 * Use replica set

 

 

Issues with following line of code: [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/core/topologies/shared.js#L231] 

Since node 12 there is no _called property as a result run timeouts cannot be cleaned up

summary: Cannot set property 'isDirty' of null
description: A user has [reported|https://github.com/Automattic/mongoose/issues/8719] seeing this error sporadically on an AWS Lambda handler. It looks like we should be gating all use of the session by its {{hasEnded}} property.

summary: ReadPreference.fromOptions doesn't pull "maxStalenessSeconds" from options
description:
I'm looking at ReadPreference in master & 3.6

[https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/src/read_preference.ts#L93-L113]

I noticed two things wrong here:

1. maxStalenessSeconds isn't pulled from options

2. hedge isn't supposed to be in options

summary: Regression using hasNext on cursor with limit
readPreferenceTags are not interpreted as an array
description: A single instance of {{readPreferenceTags}} in a connection string is parsed as an object rather than an array of size one. This breaks server selection by tag set for the unified topology.

summary: TypeError on server available with useUnifiedTopology: true
description:
I was testing with the new topology mechanism and encountered an error.

The idea is to execute the query if the server is available or throw an error after a timeout if not available.

Here is the code i used :
{code:javascript}const MongoClient = require("mongodb");
const main = async () => {
  const client = await MongoClient("mongodb://127.0.0.1", { useUnifiedTopology: true });
  const coll = client.db("test").collection("test");
  setInterval(async () => {    try {      console.log(await coll.findOne({}));    } catch (err) {        console.log(err);    }  }, 1000);};
main();
{code}
And the output :
{noformat}// [server is up, queries are running fine]null
null
null
null
null
null
null
null
null
null
null
null// [stopped the server, coll.findOne is pending with serverSelectionTimeoutMS]// [started back up the server before the serverSelectionTimeoutMS expired]
/Users/hay/Desktop/nodejs-mongo-driver/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/sdam/topology.js:1001
    if (waitQueueMember[kCancelled]) {
                       ^

TypeError: Cannot read property 'Symbol(cancelled)' of undefined
    at processWaitQueue (/Users/hay/Desktop/nodejs-mongo-driver/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/sdam/topology.js:1001:24)
    at NativeTopology.serverUpdateHandler (/Users/hay/Desktop/nodejs-mongo-driver/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/sdam/topology.js:550:7)
    at Server.emit (events.js:321:20)
    at Monitor.<anonymous> (/Users/hay/Desktop/nodejs-mongo-driver/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/sdam/server.js:147:12)
    at Monitor.emit (events.js:333:22)
    at successHandler (/Users/hay/Desktop/nodejs-mongo-driver/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/sdam/monitor.js:154:13)
    at /Users/hay/Desktop/nodejs-mongo-driver/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/sdam/monitor.js:196:5
    at callback (/Users/hay/Desktop/nodejs-mongo-driver/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/connection/connect.js:97:5)
    at /Users/hay/Desktop/nodejs-mongo-driver/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/connection/connect.js:169:5
    at /Users/hay/Desktop/nodejs-mongo-driver/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/connection/connect.js:334:7
{noformat}

summary: CMAP Connection type does not provide host/port properties
description: The kerberos module depends on these properties in order to compute the principal used for authentication.

summary: TypeError when transaction is rejected with empty reason
Fix crc32 function caching
description: The crc32 function is never defined so errors when called in deserializer.js

summary: Long integers serialize as doubles
Add support for BigInt
description: The bson module should add support for the built-in {{BigInt}} type. Currently the type is incorrectly serialized in the best case, and silently ignored in the worst case (when used for {{_id}}).

summary: Remove fnv1a from ObjectId
description: Since the spec for ObjectID never wound up using fnv1a, we should remove it so that we can eliminate the huge number of tests related to it.

summary: Use server error codes in place of error messages when possible
description: See DRIVERS-1323 for updated details.

summary: Following the "Browser (no bundling)" in the README leads to missing "global"
Incorrect imports in ESM bundle
description:
I first created this as an issue on the public GitHub repository: [https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/371].

I believe the bson package's esm bundles are suffering from a bug originating in the [rollup-plugin-commonjs|https://www.npmjs.com/package/rollup-plugin-commonjs] dependency (now [@rollup/plugin-commonjs|https://www.npmjs.com/package/@rollup/plugin-commonjs] - I verified that the bug is still present if upgrading to the latest version of this new package).

I believe the root cause is that describe in the ["External imports in the CommonJS plugin" sub-section of this issue on the Rollup.js repository|https://github.com/rollup/plugins/issues/481]:
{quote}**� Bug 5:** For external imports, this plugin will always require the default import.
{quote}
Because of this, when "long" and "buffer" is imported via `require("long")` and `require("buffer")` in the various sourcefiles and [gets translated to `import long from "long"` and `import buffer from "buffer"`|http://unpkg.com/bson@4.0.4/dist/bson.esm.js] by the commonjs plugin, it produces an invalid bundle, since "buffer" does provide a `default` export.

 
----
This results in a downstream issue when trying to bundle this package into my own IIFE bundle, using Rollup.

I've attached is some code to reproduce the issue that I'm seeing.
h3. Output from running `npx rollup -c`
{code:java}> rollup -cmain.js → bundle.js...
[!] Error: 'default' is not exported by node_modules/long/src/long.js, imported by node_modules/bson/dist/bson.esm.js
https://rollupjs.org/guide/en/#error-name-is-not-exported-by-module
node_modules/bson/dist/bson.esm.js (1:7)
1: import long from 'long';
          ^
2: import buffer from 'buffer';
Error: 'default' is not exported by node_modules/long/src/long.js, imported by node_modules/bson/dist/bson.esm.js
    at error (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:5171:30)
    at Module.error (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:9603:16)
    at handleMissingExport (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:9525:28)
    at Module.traceVariable (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:9986:24)
    at ModuleScope.findVariable (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:8549:39)
    at MemberExpression.bind (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:6324:49)
    at AssignmentExpression.bind (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:2880:23)
    at ExpressionStatement$1.bind (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:2880:23)
    at Program$1.bind (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:2876:31)
    at Module.bindReferences (/Users/kraenhansen/Projects/bson-bug/node_modules/rollup/dist/shared/rollup.js:9599:18){code}

summary: Support parsing $uuid as extended JSON representation for subtype 4 binary
description: See DRIVERS-848 for updated details.

summary: Not using template string for ssl error message
MongoError: Not Master when running createIndex in 3.6.0
Improve Cursor Documentation in native
merge `Cursor#hasNext` into core cursor logic
description: The implementation of {{Cursor#hasNext}} manually decrements the {{cursorIndex}} and {{currentLimit}} state variables of the core cursor. The method should be moved to the core {{Cursor}} implementation.

summary: Only allow promiseLibrary on MongoClient
description: We should only allow users to pass in a promise library to MongoClient, and stop documenting it on individual methods.

summary: Remove de-structuring from 3.6 branch
Missing function overload type for `EJSON.stringify`
Update sdam monitoring tests with directConnection uri option
description: See DRIVERS-1249 for updated details.

summary: Use peerDependenciesMeta field to mark peer optional dependencies
description: Our non-standard use of {{peerOptionalDependencies}} is causing trouble for users of package managers with stricter guidelines around dependency management (yarn, pnpm). We can use a newly introduced {{peerDependenciesMeta}} field to inform these package managers of the optional nature of these driver dependencies.

summary: ignoreUndefined not working for find*Replace find*Modify methods
description:
With upgrade to 3.* we have modified all our calls to updateOne to replace whole document (as updates now can't take whole document) with findOneAndReplace.

But these methods don't honor the setting ignoreUndefined causing severe issues in production applications.

Please fix this at the earliest and issue a release.

Have gone through collection.js and actullay found this option check missing. There should be an elegant way to handle this rather than check with each method.

summary: Deprecate geoHaystack and geoSearch
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
        {panel:title=Downstream Change Summary|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}
    geoHaystack and geoSearch are now deprecated
    {panel}
    h2. Description of Linked Ticket
    null
    
    See DRIVERS-987 for updated details.

summary: GSSAPI AuthProvider causing crashes in Compass
description:
Compass crashes after a successful authentication to kerberos.

The authentication with kerberos gets triggered multiple times inside the driver on MongoClient.connect, however the GSSPI code in the driver is not meant to deal with concurrent calls as that seems to break the state of the authentication completely.

Doing a serialisation of the authentication and reusing the same state for the same requests seems to fix it.

The setup to reproduce the issue and a possible fix is in this gist:
https://gist.github.com/mcasimir/1d89b8fa81c8fed51f387f394abe6c51

summary: Performance Regression for usage of mongodb connections (queries, inserts, ...)
description:
The fix for [https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/NODE-2803] introduced a noticable performance regression for all mongodb connections (queries, inserts, ...).

This performance hit is only visible, if the nodejs event loop is under pressure.

In the example in the linked ticket nothing happens but mongodb insert logic. This results in an average iteration duration of the event loop of around 1ms.

==> the setImmediate usage results in a 1ms delay before sending the request to mongodb (compared to 3.6.1)

 

But if the nodejs process (for example as a webserver) has multiple further things to do on the event loop (everything shortlived, fast, non blocking) this average iteration duration of the event loop can rise. For example to ~10ms.

==> the setImmediate usage results in a ~10ms delay before sending the request to mongodb (compared to 3.6.1) to get the next batch

 

This small delay adds up, most noticable for queries with cursors and multiple getMore-Roundtrips to Mongodb.

summary: Invalid assignment of X509 username makes authentication impossible
description: An erroneous use of {{Object.apply}} rather than {{Object.assign}} makes it so users cannot provide a username when performing X509 authentication.

summary: Pound sign (#) breaks URI parsing in connection string
description:
*The Problem*

Passwords containing a pound sign (#) aka hashtag cause this error in the NodeJS library. The error is not generated in the C# library nor the mongoimport command line if you use a literal #.
{code:JavaScript}const client = new MongoClient("mongodb+srv://connectUser:pass#word@mycluster-mallr.azure.mongodb.net/mydb?retryWrites=true&w=majority", { useNewUrlParser: true, useUnifiedTopology: true });{code}
 
{code}Database error: MongoParseError: URI does not have hostname, domain name and tld
 at parseSrvConnectionString (app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/uri_parser.js:41:21)
 at parseConnectionString (app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/uri_parser.js:556:12)
 at connect (app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/operations/connect.js:272:3)
 at app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/mongo_client.js:218:5
 at maybePromise (app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/utils.js:719:3)
 at MongoClient.connect (app/node_modules/mongodb/lib/mongo_client.js:214:10)
 at module.exports (app/app/index.js:51:22)
 at WorkerChannel.invocationRequest (/usr/lib/azure-functions-core-tools-3/workers/node/worker-bundle.js:17928:26)
 at ClientDuplexStream.<anonymous> (/usr/lib/azure-functions-core-tools-3/workers/node/worker-bundle.js:17738:30)
 at ClientDuplexStream.emit (events.js:310:20)
{ [Symbol(mongoErrorContextSymbol)]: {} }
{code}
 

*The Workaround*

Manually escape # with %23. This should work fine everywhere BUT the same URI should ideally work idempotently across every MongoDB library. If some support literal # and some do not, that is a bug...

 

*The Documentation*

[https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/connection-string/]

The documentation only cautions about _"the at sign {{@}}, colon {{:}}, slash {{/}}, or the percent sign {{%}} character"_ although the reference that it links to, [RFC3986|https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3986#section-2.1], does mention # as being problematic if you understand what IETF is getting at...

If you wish for your documentation to be updated, then the other libraries need to be fixed to disallow literal # in order to be consistent.

 

_*Thank you for your work on this project!!!*_

summary: Deprecate Topology events in `Db`
description: Before we introduce the breaking change, we should deprecate setting event listeners for topology events on the Db with some annoying deprecation messages.

summary: Revisions to "Issues and Help" pages of drivers documentation
description:
h4. Description of Drivers Ticket:
    Example: https://mongodb.github.io/mongo-csharp-driver/2.2/issues_help/
1) doesn't include links to support team (support.mongodb.com)
2) link to community forum is no longer accurate (it's now community.mongodb.com)
3) we could include a link to file a feature request (feedback.mongodb.com)

We should go through all drivers and make sure any help/assistance pages have the correct links/directions.


    See DRIVERS-1180 for updated details.

summary: Rename BulkWriteError to MongoBulkWriteError
description: This error is not named like the rest of our error types, rename it.

summary: official doc references wrong MongoClient.open instead of MongoClient.connect
description: in the official documentacion https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/api-generated/mongoclient.html there is a code snippet that references to mongoclient.open, but this function does not exist. it should be mongoclient.connect, instead. please, correct it. thank you.

summary: Legacy topology hangs with unlimited socket timeout
description: The change to make {{socketTimeoutMS}} default to 0 (infinity) resulted in a number of tests hanging with the legacy topology types. This ticket tracks work to fix those issues, and return the tests to green

summary: bson does not expose a default export
description:
When loading {{bson}} from TypeScript as {{import bson from 'bson';}}, that breaks with bson@4.2.0, because TypeScript sees that `lib/bson.js` was generated from a TypeScript module without a default export. (This does not affect genuine ES6 modules, only TypeScript modules.)

Adding a default export back, at least for bson@4.x.x, should fix this.

summary: Inspection method for Binary does not yield same result when evaluated
